tooltip.culinarycompat.guidebook.subtitl: A guide to perfectly managing ye' olde kitchen
tooltip.culinarycompat:ae2_kitchen_stati: Wirelessly integrates ME networks with CFB Kitchens!
Link the Kitchen Station using a Wireless Connector. See the §6Head Chef's Journal§r§7 for more info...
culinarycompat.guide.name: Head Chef's Journal
culinarycompat.guide.landing: I've been a chef for many years, managing my kitchen only got harder over these long years... I've been getting bloody fed up of dealing with the Donuts I have to call employees.$(br2)$(o)I hope my notes over all this time served me well...$()
category.culinarycompat.ae2.name: Applied Energistics
category.culinarycompat.ae2.description: Connects $(6)Cooking For Blockheads$() to $(5)Applied Energistics 2$() ME networks. Adds the ME Kitchen Station block.
entry.culinarycompat.ae2.kitchen_station: The $(l)ME Kitchen Station$() is all you need to connect $(6)CFB$() with $(5)Applied Energistics 2$().$(br2)Configure it in an $(5)AE2$() ME Security Terminal as if it were a Wireless Terminal. Then place it as part of your $(6)CFB$() kitchen multi-block. You can now craft using your $(5)AE2$() network inventory.
$(br2)See the next pages for screen color reference.
When not connected to a network, the screen is black.
When linked to an active network, the screen turns purple.

Right-click the $(l)ME Kitchen Station$() to open its settings panel. A bit about the three: $(l)Mode$(), $(l)Screen$(), and the $(l)Priority$() wrench tab in the upper-right.$(br2)$(l)Screen$() flips the block face on or off. Purely cosmetic, the bridge runs either way regardless of what the screen shows.

$(l)Mode$() picks which way items flow between the kitchen and the $(5)AE2$() network.$(br2)$(3)ME → Kitchen$()$(br)Recipes inside $(6)CFB$() draw ingredients from your $(5)AE2$() network. Kitchen cabinets and fridges stay hidden from $(5)AE2$().$(br2)$(6)Kitchen → ME$()$(br)Multiblock storage shows up in your $(5)AE2$() terminal. Cooking does $(o)not$() pull from the network.

$(2)Bidirectional$() (default)$(br)Both directions at once. The kitchen draws from $(5)AE2$() for cooking, and kitchen storage shows up on $(5)AE2$() too.$(br2)Most setups want $(2)Bidirectional$(). Pick a one-way mode if you need to wall the kitchen off from auto-craft routing or want strict separation between cooking ingredients and network inventory.

The wrench tab in the upper-right opens the $(5)AE2$() priority page.$(br2)$(l)Higher$() priority storage receives inserts first; $(l)lower$() priority gets extracted from first. Same priority system used by $(5)AE2$() storage cells and storage buses.$(br2)The priority system is the reason the BIDIRECTIONAL mode exists!
